psalm-79-1.prayer: Lord, when sacred things are broken or dishonored, help me grieve with faith and trust that You are still present and sovereign.

psalm-79-2.verse:
They have given the dead bodies of Your servants for food to the birds of the heavens,
  The flesh of Your godly ones to the beasts of the earth.
psalm-79-2.prayer: Father, protect Your people and bring dignity, mercy, and care where there has been loss, suffering, or destruction.

psalm-79-3.verse:
They have poured out their blood like water round about Jerusalem;
  And there was no one to bury them.
psalm-79-3.prayer: Lord, when sorrow surrounds me, help me bring my pain honestly before You and trust Your compassion.

psalm-79-4.verse:
We have become a reproach to our neighbors,
  A scoffing and derision to those around us.
psalm-79-4.prayer: Father, when I feel mocked, rejected, or misunderstood, remind me that my identity is secure in You.

psalm-79-5.verse:
How long, O Lord? Will You be angry forever?
  Will Your jealousy burn like fire?
psalm-79-5.prayer: Lord, when hardship feels long and overwhelming, strengthen my patience and renew my hope in Your mercy.

psalm-79-6.verse:
Pour out Your wrath upon the nations which do not know You,
  And upon the kingdoms which do not call upon Your name.
psalm-79-6.prayer: Father, bring justice where Your name is rejected and Your truth is ignored. Help me trust Your righteous judgment.

psalm-79-7.verse:
For they have devoured Jacob
  And laid waste his habitation.
psalm-79-7.prayer: Lord, restore what has been damaged by sin, rebellion, or destruction. Let Your mercy bring healing and renewal.

psalm-79-8.prayer: Father, do not remember past sins against me. Meet me with compassion and help me walk in repentance and grace.

psalm-79-9.verse:
Help us, O God of our salvation, for the glory of Your name;
  And deliver us and forgive our sins for Your name’s sake.
psalm-79-9.prayer: Lord, help me for the glory of Your name. Deliver me, forgive me, and let my life reflect Your mercy and salvation.

psalm-79-10.verse:
Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?”
  Let there be known among the nations in our sight,
  Vengeance for the blood of Your servants which has been shed.
psalm-79-10.prayer: Father, let Your name be honored among all people. Show Your faithfulness so others may know that You are God.

psalm-79-11.verse:
Let the groaning of the prisoner come before You;
  According to the greatness of Your power preserve those who are doomed to die.
psalm-79-11.prayer: Lord, hear the groaning of those who suffer. Bring freedom, mercy, and hope to those who feel trapped or condemned.

psalm-79-12.verse:
And return to our neighbors sevenfold into their bosom
  The reproach with which they have reproached You, O Lord.
psalm-79-12.prayer: Father, deal with injustice according to Your wisdom. Help me leave vengeance in Your hands and walk in faithfulness.

psalm-79-13.prayer: Lord, thank You for making us the sheep of Your pasture. Help me give thanks to You forever and declare Your praise to every generation.

psalm-79-closing.verse:
Lord, thank You for being merciful, just, and faithful even in seasons of sorrow, loss, and confusion. Help me trust You when life feels broken, seek Your forgiveness with humility, and rely on Your compassion. Restore what has been damaged, defend Your name, and teach me to praise You through every generation. May my life remain grateful, faithful, and anchored in Your salvation.

In Jesus’ name, Amen.
psalm-79-closing.prayer:
Lord, thank You for being merciful, just, and faithful even in seasons of sorrow, loss, and confusion. Help me trust You when life feels broken, seek Your forgiveness with humility, and rely on Your compassion. Restore what has been damaged, defend Your name, and teach me to praise You through every generation. May my life remain grateful, faithful, and anchored in Your salvation.

In Jesus’ name, Amen.
